title: [Bug] No duplicate order ID detection — can corrupt order book state
body:
## Problem

The engine does not check for duplicate order IDs. Submitting two orders with the same ID causes both to exist in the book. When `CancelOrder` is called, only the first one is removed. The second becomes an orphan that can still match.

```go
e.SubmitOrder("BTC", model.NewLimitOrder("dup", model.Sell, 100, 5))
e.SubmitOrder("BTC", model.NewLimitOrder("dup", model.Sell, 200, 10))
// Both exist in the book
e.CancelOrder("BTC", "dup") // Only removes the first one
```

This also means `CancelOrder` behavior is unpredictable — it depends on which order appears first in the slice.

## Affected Files
- `pkg/engine/engine.go` — `SubmitOrder()` lacks ID uniqueness check
- `pkg/orderbook/orderbook.go` — `RemoveOrder()` stops at first match

## Proposed Fix
- Maintain an `orders map[string]*model.Order` index in the engine or order book
- Reject orders with duplicate IDs in `SubmitOrder()` with an error
- Use the map for O(1) cancel lookups instead of linear scan

## Priority
High — data integrity issue.
